scope: Pass B review of Jira Cloud REST API v3. The bundle keeps the legacy read-only sync contracts for issue search, project search, and user search. Jira's remaining REST API groups are either detail duplicates, parameterized/fan-out subresources, administrative/configuration metadata, binary attachment payloads, or mutating workflows that are not exposed by the legacy connector.

excluded.reason: Issue detail returns the same issue object family as the covered issue search stream and requires caller-supplied issue IDs instead of producing an independent collection.
Project and user detail endpoints duplicate the covered project/user search streams and require explicit IDs/accountIds.
Authenticated-user profile introspection is used only by the Check health-check request, not a syncable object stream.
Issue subresources require per-issue fan-out from the issue stream, which is not expressible as a standalone declarative stream without a StreamHook.
Attachment content and thumbnails return binary payloads; this connector syncs structured JSON records only.
Project subresources require project-ID fan-out or separate schemas outside the legacy project-search record contract.
Administrative and configuration endpoints often require Jira admin scopes/permissions and are outside the default source-read contract.
Discovery/configuration helpers and non-issue business metadata are not part of legacy's emitted issue/project/user data surface.
POST search variants expose the same JQL issue search family as the covered GET /search stream; the bundle keeps the legacy GET search shape.
Create/comment/transition/worklog endpoints mutate Jira project data and require a dedicated reverse-ETL approval/risk design; legacy Write is unsupported.
Update endpoints mutate Jira issues, comments, worklogs, project configuration, or workflow metadata and are intentionally not exposed by this read-only connector.
Delete endpoints remove Jira business data or administrative configuration and are intentionally not exposed.
